what_it_does: GC4 floor-triggered re-ask pass. For each entry in reconciled.json classed 'floor' (the deterministic fact-checker caught a fabrication), it rebuilds the original element unit, re-fires the register_element role with the same grounding context plus the floor's named rejection reasons as an explicit correction, then re-runs the floor (refcheck) + jury (confirm_registration) + confirm_status to reclassify it. Updates reconciled.json in place, preserving the prior dossier and stamping reasked:true. Then retires the prior STATUS card and surfaces a final reconciled card.
observations: Loads register_element and confirm_registration from the role registry; builds a Role variant reg_v with input_addresses extended to (utterance, ground, exemplars, inventory).
Loads confirm_registration via importlib spec_from_file_location to get confirm_status directly from roles/confirm_registration.py.
Groups floor entries by source mockup so the shared grounding context is resolved once per group.

Filters floor reasons to only those starting with 'refcheck' and injects a verbatim correction: maps_to_feature must be copied verbatim or be the literal 'proposed', capabilities must use only allowed words.
Runs register_element via C.run_items, then per-item runs C.run_jury for confirmation; reclassifies to confirmed / floor / jury based on confirmed flag and whether refcheck reasons reappear.
Skips entries with no artifact or an unresolvable cluster rep, recording the reason.
Recounts all classes and persists in place (no-versioning law).
Imports sys but does not appear to use it beyond path insertion.
standouts: The core idea: the deterministic floor's rejection reason IS the corrective feedback fed back for one bounded re-fire — closing the loop between detection and repair.
Maximal-capture history: prior_dossier kept, reask_failed recorded, reasked stamped — nothing silently overwritten.
A re-asked dossier passes only by passing the SAME floor + jury — the gate is not relaxed for the second attempt.
conventions: Follows the propose-only floor discipline (operator's resolved field never touched).
In-place update honoring the stated no-versioning law.
Same sys.path / chdir bootstrapping as siblings rg10_redescribe/rg10_rejury.
Builds a role variant by copying spec and extending input_addresses — a recurring pattern across this pipeline.
concerns: Two different mechanisms load confirm_registration: rr.get('confirm_registration') for jury and importlib for confirm_status — two handles to one role, easy to drift.
Bounded to ONE re-fire by design; stubborn fabrications stay floor with no further automated recovery (intended, but a hard ceiling).
The live-card retire/surface side effects are coupled into the same script as the reclassification pass.
notes: GC4 re-ask: feeds the fact-checker's own rejection reasons back to register_element for one bounded retry, re-gates, and reclassifies floor-caught fabrications in place.
questions: Where is the s97 STATUS card retire / final-card surface logic referenced in the docstring — the visible main() doesn't show that surfacing step?
What exactly does confirm_status return (status/confirmed/jury/reasons) and how does its jury differ from C.run_jury's verdict?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly match — the re-ask, re-gate, in-place reclassification, and history capture are all present; the docstring's final live-card retire/surface step is described but not visible in the shown main(), so that part may live elsewhere or be omitted.
apparent_themes: Detection reason as repair signal (the floor's no is the feedback).
Bounded autonomous refinement before the human gate.
Gate is never relaxed for retries.
intention_signals: Tries to recover fixable fabrications automatically by treating the deterministic fact-check's complaint as a precise correction prompt, so the operator's eventual gate receives fewer flagged items while stubborn cases stay loudly flagged.
novelty: Feeding a deterministic checker's rejection reasons back into the generative role as an explicit verbatim correction, then re-gating through the identical floor, is a distinctive self-repair loop.
contribution: Contributes the closed-loop re-ask repair stage — the mechanism that turns floor-caught fabrications into a bounded corrective re-fire and reclassifies them in place with full history.
summary_for_embedding: A Python pass that re-fires the register_element role on fabrication-flagged registry entries using the fact-checker's own rejection reasons as a verbatim correction, re-gates them, and reclassifies in place.
intention_for_embedding: To automatically repair fixable fabricated registry dossiers by feeding the deterministic floor's reasons back for one bounded retry, shrinking the human review set.
